title: Metric selection pitfalls
description: Exact-match, BLEU/ROUGE overlap, and accuracy can each diverge from real quality and be gamed without improving the system.
body: Use when picking a scoring metric. Exact-match penalizes correct paraphrases; n-gram overlap (BLEU/ROUGE) rewards surface similarity over meaning; raw accuracy hides class imbalance and severity. Each can be optimized while user-facing quality stagnates or drops. Do not pick a metric for convenience, and avoid a single blended number that hides segment failures. Actions: choose metrics that correlate with the goal, pair automatic metrics with human spot-checks, and report per-segment. Validation signals include a measured correlation between the metric and human judgment. Risk boundary: optimizing a gameable metric ships a hidden regression; when metric and human disagree, trust the human.
